cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out the answer to the question about why different colors of light were used in the film Titanic. Let me read through the provided text again to make sure I understand the context. The passage mentions that in Titanic, the lighting is very important. The poor, lower class people are always shown in very warm-coloured light that gives them a sense of life and happiness. On the other hand, the rich, upper class are always shown in a flat light portraying the feeling of hardness and coldness. The text says this is done to show the issue of class segregation and create stereotypes. So, the different colors of light are used to highlight the differences between the social classes. The warm light for the lower class and the flat/cold light for the upper class. The question is asking why different colors of light were used. Looking at the options: A) They were used to show the difference between the rich and the poor. B) The director was determined to achieve good visual effect. C) The social status was represented by the color of light in the past. D) The use of light was the main technique the director made use of. The text specifically states that the lighting is used to show class segregation. Option A directly states that the different colors of light were used to show the difference between the rich and the poor. This matches what is written in the passage. Option B mentions visual effects, but the text is focusing on the thematic and social commentary aspect rather than just aesthetics. Option C talks about how social status was represented in the past, but the text is explaining how the film uses lighting to portray this, not that it was a past practice.
